Document:
# Guildmaster
The Guildmaster leads the Mercenary Guild. They organize contracts, manage Guild Workers, guide Adventurers, and represent the guild's interests in the settlement. A Guildmaster's field experience makes them respected — and their authority makes them effective.
## Responsibilities
- Lead the Mercenary Guild and set its priorities - Accept and manage contracts for Adventurers - Oversee Guild Workers and their base operations - Negotiate with other factions and players on behalf of the guild - Respond to serious threats when the guild's interests are at stake
## Requirements
[bold]5 hours[/bold] as an Adventurer. Salary: [bold]200 coins[/bold] per cycle.
Your arrival as Guildmaster is announced to the administration.
## Tips
- Set clear contracts for Adventurers at the start of the round — targets, rewards, priorities - Guild Workers handle logistics, so delegate base tasks accordingly - Your salary is the highest in the Mercenary department — use that influence to keep the guild cohesive - Stay connected to both the field (Adventurers) and the base (Guild Workers) to keep operations running
